summary:
Recovery: Excellent

Reading: The training window's closing on a day you set aside to recover, and that was the right call — your body ran on short sleep last night and you've still been on your feet most of the day, so there's nothing to prove tonight. You tend to hold up best when you let recovery actually land instead of forcing output through fatigue, and right now the smart move is feeding the rebuild — your protein's barely past halfway with a real gap to close before bed, and that's the one number that decides whether today's rest protects muscle or quietly costs you. Knees and joints are quiet, nothing nagging — just get the protein in, water down, and aim for a longer night than you got.

Performance: Wind down. The window's closed and you kept it a true rest day — close the protein gap, get to bed earlier than last night, and tomorrow opens as a fresh platform to train on.

Transit: none
